content: The tree must handle spec-drop pairs where the user turn contains a complete design document and the agent turn is just acknowledgement. The current design assumes agent proposes / user confirms, but spec drops invert this — the user turn is the substantive one. A single user turn may contain multiple distinct decisions (e.g. seq 10 had ~8 decisions in one message). The tree is designed for one signal per pair detection but must produce multiple proposals from a single pair when warranted.

provenance.raw_agent: Edge case worth noting: seq 10 — a single user turn containing a complete design document. That's not really a 'pair' in the ratification sense, it's a spec drop. The tree needs to handle the case where the user turn is the substantive one and the agent turn is just acknowledgement.
provenance.raw_user: I think it's fine. We can also look at .pi/inbox to try to judge what would make sense.
provenance.what_was_ratified: The triage analysis is correct. The tree needs to handle spec-drop pairs where the user turn is the substantive content and a single pair can produce multiple proposals.
